brand.name: Coach
brand.description: American leather goods house founded in New York City in 1941. Mass-accessible 'premium' tier (not luxury) under Tapestry, Inc. Most commonly found designer handbag brand at US thrift stores and Goodwill, making it a high-volume authentication and discovery target.

style.name: Tabby
style.description: The Tabby traces to a 1970s-era design lineage associated with legendary Coach designer Bonnie Cashin, though the specific silhouette sold today is not a direct 1:1 reissue of a single original model -- sourcing on the exact original product is thin. Creative director Stuart Vevers relaunched the Tabby as a modern style in Fall 2019, and it became one of Coach's signature turn-lock bags, sold in multiple sizes (commonly 26 and Shoulder Bag configurations) and many limited-edition material/print collaborations.
style.notes: year_introduced=1976 reflects the design lineage cited by fashion press (Bonnie Cashin era); this is less firmly verified than the 2019 relaunch date, which is corroborated by multiple sources. Treat 1976 as approximate/contextual, not a confirmed first-production year for an item called 'Tabby'.

variants.strap_type: both (detachable short strap and detachable long strap)
variants.interior_material: fabric lining (varies by version; some leather versions use suede lining)
variants.authentication_markers: Turn-lock should feel solid/heavy, not hollow or light; engraving on hardware should be clean, centered, and deep, not shallow or blurry. Dimensions approx 10.25" (L) x 5.5"-6.75" (H) x 2.75"-4.25" (D) depending on material/version. Detachable long strap drop commonly cited around 21"-21.5", short strap drop around 7".
variants.notes: Retail price set to $450 USD, the better-corroborated current figure (AOL editorial 'Brass/Chalk ... $450.00', Macy's, eBay NWT $450); an earlier note had $475, which appeared in only one unverified summary and may reflect a quilted/Pillow variant ($495-$575). Coach frequently runs limited print/material collabs under the same 'Tabby Shoulder Bag 26' name with different SKUs (e.g. CH857, CCC02, CI032, CP150), so exact dimensions/price vary slightly by specific drop. This record represents the standard leather version. Verify on live coach.com to upgrade confidence.

variants.size_label: Tabby Shoulder Bag 26 in Signature Canvas
variants.exterior_material: coated canvas (signature canvas) with calf leather trim
variants.exterior_colorway: Signature jacquard print, varies by season
variants.strap_type: both (detachable short strap and detachable long strap)
variants.authentication_markers: Combines signature coated canvas with refined calf leather trim; features inside zip and multifunction pockets, snap closure, outside slip pocket. Detachable short strap 7" drop, detachable long strap 21" drop. Dimensions approx 10.25" (L) x 6" (H) x 3.25" (W).
variants.notes: Retail price for this specific canvas SKU (CI032) not confirmed in research; left null rather than guessed.

production_records.screw_engraving: Back of the C-closure plate carries an engraved hardware batch code, with the top of the digits partly tucked under the leather (single-source, verify physically).
production_records.date_code_format: Modern Coach (post-2014) generally does NOT print a decodable month/year date code. The Tabby's interior creed carries a STYLE number, not a date code -- letter+digits (e.g. CH857) or legacy 5-digit numeric (e.g. 73995). Suffix letters after a hyphen: F = factory/outlet exclusive, M = Macy's exclusive, N = Nordstrom exclusive.
production_records.stamp_placement: Style number stamped at the bottom of a square leather creed patch, stitched inside the main interior compartment. Unlike softer modern Coach styles (which moved the number to a sewn-in white fabric tag c.2014), the structured Tabby retains a stamped leather creed.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Font must be crisp and evenly spaced; faded or poorly-printed text is a red flag. Counterfeit creed patches are often undersized with too little space around the embossed border outline and text.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Turn-lock 'C' engraving should be clean, thick, and deeply etched -- shallow/messy = replica. Back of the C-closure is a heavy gold-tone plate held by real screws with an engraved hardware batch code. Hardware should feel substantial (heft test); brassy gold tone. Stitching even and secure; creed must be stitched, not glued. KNOWN FAKE TELL: a creed reading 'CR652' belongs to the Coach Juliet, not the Tabby. Verified real Tabby 26 style codes from coach.com: CH857, CCC02, CP150, 73995, 76105 (colorblock), C0772 (Pillow Tabby 26). Documented countries of manufacture for modern Coach: Vietnam, Cambodia, Philippines, India (Tapestry FY2025 10-K), plus China in FY2019/earlier; country alone does not authenticate.

production_records.date_code_format: Same modern-Coach creed system as the leather Tabby: style number (letter+digits or legacy 5-digit) on a stamped leather creed, no decodable month/year date code post-2014.
production_records.stamp_placement: Style number stamped at the bottom of a square leather creed patch, stitched inside the main interior compartment.
production_records.stamp_font_notes: Font crisp and evenly spaced; creed stitched, not glued.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Signature coated canvas with refined calf-leather trim and brass/gold-tone hardware (SKU CI032). Same turn-lock and creed authentication checks as the leather Tabby. CI032 dimensions were internally consistent across retailer snippets at 10.25" L x 6" H x 3.25" D.

known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: Standard polished pebble leather line ('Brass/Chalk'). Interior lining color not stated in any accessible source. Source: StockX, AOL, Macy's (snippet-level).
Polished pebble leather. HARDWARE TONE CONFLICT: some listings show gold/brass, one summary shows silver/nickel -- both versions may exist. hardware_color left null pending confirmation.
Signature 'C' coated canvas with calf-leather trim. Source: coach.com, Haute24, Dillard's (snippet-level).
Polished pebble leather. Source: Amazon listing (snippet-level).

carry_methods.notes: Via detachable long strap, commonly cited drop of approximately 21"-21.5" (53-55cm), suitable for crossbody wear.
Via detachable short strap, approximately 7" (17.8cm) drop, for shoulder/handheld carry.
Top handle / hand-carry possible when straps are detached, per product description of the Tabby's multi-carry design.

fits.notes: Multiple owner reviews carry a Pro Max comfortably, often in the back slip pocket. Source: Emtalks, Ella Pretty Blog.

fits.item_name: Compact wallet (or even a long wallet)
fits.notes: BrandsShoppe: 'large enough to fit a long wallet and has a divider pocket'; corroborated by Aglaia Magazine.
Fits per Emtalks. A YouTube comparison implies AirPods + a full load is more the Tabby 36's territory, so 'yes' alone, tight when combined.
Fits but 'starts to get a little snug' alongside a full load. Source: An Indigo Day, Emtalks, BrandsShoppe.
Single aggregated source states a water bottle does not fit; consistent with the ~8cm depth and rigid build. Low confidence. Source: Amaboxly.
Aggregated source states 'not a tablet'; iPad Mini not named specifically. Low confidence. Source: Amaboxly.
Not independently verified from a source.

interior_storage.material: fabric or suede lining depending on version
interior_storage.authentication_notes: Described as 'inside zip and multifunction pockets' on canvas version; configuration may vary slightly by material/SKU.

interior_storage.placement: interior / exterior (turnlock and open pockets noted on some versions)
interior_storage.authentication_notes: Leather Tabby Turnlock Medium version described with outside turn-lock and open pockets plus 1 interior snap pocket and 1 interior zip pocket with suede lining.

serial_tags.format: Vintage/older format: two-letter factory or inspector code, slash, numeric style number, slash, two-digit year (e.g. LD/123/85). Modern format (circa 2020+): Coach moved to alphanumeric style numbers (letter + four-digit sequence, e.g. CH857), and around 2014 began phasing out stamped date codes on the creed patch entirely -- many current creed patches show only the authenticity paragraph with no numbers underneath, with style/factory info moved to a separate fabric tag sewn into the lining. NOTE: the structured Tabby is an exception -- it retains a STAMPED leather creed carrying the style number, rather than moving it to a fabric tag.
serial_tags.placement: Interior leather patch stamped inside the main compartment of the Tabby (bottom of a square leather creed patch). Vintage/other modern styles may use a fabric tag near the lining seam.
serial_tags.material: Stamped or blind-embossed leather patch (vintage and Tabby); printed fabric tag (many softer modern styles)
serial_tags.year_range: Vintage format roughly pre-1994; transitional formats through the 2010s; current alphanumeric style-number format from approximately 2020 onward
serial_tags.how_to_read: Vintage: first two letters = factory/inspector code, middle number = style number, final two digits = year of production. Modern (2020+): style numbers follow patterns such as Letter+4 digits (e.g. CH857), or Letter+4digits-Letter+4digits, or with suffixes -- an 'F' after a hyphen indicates a Factory/outlet-exclusive bag; 'M' indicates Macy's exclusive; 'N' indicates Nordstrom exclusive. Legacy 5-digit numeric style numbers (e.g. 73995, 76105) also exist for the Tabby across production years.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: Stamping should be clean, even, and crisp -- blurry or inconsistent fonts are a red flag. Placement should be in the standard designated area; placement outside the norm is suspect. The patch itself should be genuine leather with proper stitching (stitched, not glued); irregular leather or sloppy stitching suggests counterfeit. KNOWN FAKE TELL: a Tabby creed reading 'CR652' is fake -- that code belongs to the Coach Juliet Shoulder Bag. Verified real Tabby 26 codes (from coach.com): CH857, CCC02, CP150, 73995, 76105, and C0772 (Pillow Tabby 26). The serial on the interior label should match the number printed on the dust bag. Coach does not authenticate bags, so 'call Coach' is not a verification route.

locks_and_keys.authentication_notes: The Tabby's closure is an integrated turn-lock (or snap closure on canvas versions), not a separate padlock-and-key system. No source found indicating Coach Tabby ships with a lock/key set. A separate padlock accessory on a Tabby would be inconsistent with the style.

provenance_packaging.description: Coach dust bag included with new purchase, standard practice across the brand's current product lines.
provenance_packaging.material: Fabric (non-woven or flannel-style, consistent with Coach's general packaging practice)
provenance_packaging.branding: Coach logo/wordmark, exact current design not independently confirmed for Tabby specifically
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: Dust bag presence/absence does not by itself authenticate a bag; the serial on the interior creed should match the number printed on the dust bag. Specific design/branding details not verified in this research pass.
